Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying This Action
Off Calendar Nov-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS SALVADOR RODRIGUEZ, CONSTRUCTION CONCIERGE, INC.'S Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying This Action:

Defendants Salvador Rodriguez, Construction Concierge, Inc. and Ronald Hill's "motion for order compelling arbitration and staying this action" is denied, for two reasons.
First, defendants have not adduced a "written agreement to submit to arbitration" a controversy. (See CCP 1281.) One proffered document is signed by only one of the two plaintiffs and no defendant. That document has a checked box providing for mediation of disputes, after which "any party may take the matter to court." The document's next - seemingly contradictory - paragraph mentions "arbitration," but has an empty blank following that word. Another document has the appearance of a bid; it has a paragraph about "arbitration." However, this document is signed only by Rodriguez and no other defendant or either plaintiff.
Second, even treating either (or both) of these documents as an arbitration agreement, it would be void for failure to comply with Business and Professions Code 7191.

order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
